subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Staffing Cost of Cloud
description: A fast breakdown of The Staffing Cost of Cloud for builders.

hook.text: Cloud engineers earn $150,000+ per year. The cloud promised to reduce IT costs — but salaries keep rising.

buildup.text: Cloud didn't eliminate IT jobs. It changed them. You need architects, DevOps engineers, and security specialists.

discovery.text: Companies that migrate to cloud without training their teams end up hiring expensive consultants instead.

twist.text: The cloud skills gap is real. Demand for cloud engineers far exceeds supply, driving salaries even higher.

climax.text: Cloud infrastructure is cheaper. Cloud talent is not. Staffing is often the largest hidden cost of cloud adoption.

punchline.text: Cloud saves on hardware. It spends on people.

quiz.question: Why is staffing often the largest hidden cost of cloud adoption?
options: Cloud requires specialized engineers whose salaries far exceed traditional IT roles
Cloud providers require companies to hire their certified staff
Cloud systems need 10 times more staff than on-premises
